content:
- Marked story-02 task-01 done with passes true and checked the completed acceptance items plus plan slices.
- Ran `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, which refreshed Ralph summaries and sent the completion email.
- Next I am reviewing the final git status, then staging all files, committing with test evidence, and pushing.

- Read prior progress log entry showing story-02 task-01 was completed and task switch already ran.
- Read current active and done task summaries; current active chain starts at story-02 task-02, and story-02 task-01 is done with passes true.
- Next I am refreshing the Ralph summary, checking chooser rules, and confirming the actual code stack/architecture before writing current_task.txt.

- Refreshed Ralph task summaries and re-read chooser rules.
- Confirmed actual stack/architecture from codebase: Go module `pg_gobench`, stdlib-style CLI entrypoint in `cmd/pg_gobench`, internal app/config/database/httpserver packages, Makefile using gofmt/go vet/go test.
- Confirmed there are no active bug tasks in the current summary and only one unblocked failing task remains: `.ralph/tasks/story-02-control-plane/task-02-run-coordinator.md`; next I will write that path to `.ralph/current_task.txt` and stop.

- Wrote `.ralph/current_task.txt` with the selected next task path.
- Selected `.ralph/tasks/story-02-control-plane/task-02-run-coordinator.md` because story-02 task-01 is already done, there are no active bug tasks in the current summary, and all later tasks remain blocked by this chain.
- Next worker should execute story-02 task-02 with the required TDD workflow and then re-run task selection after completion.

- Read Ralph progress log and confirmed the selected task is `.ralph/tasks/story-02-control-plane/task-02-run-coordinator.md`.
- Read the required `tdd` and `improve-code-boundaries` skills to follow the mandated workflow before touching code.
- Next I am reading the task file and story directory to determine whether the task is still in planning/verification state or ready for execution.

- Read the task file and confirmed this turn must stay in planning mode because task-02 has no plan yet.
- Inspected task-01’s completed plan and task markup to match the repository’s Ralph workflow and plan-file conventions.
- Next I am reading the current code boundaries in `internal/benchmark`, `internal/app`, and `internal/httpserver` so the coordinator plan matches the existing architecture.

- Read the current app/server/benchmark code to design the coordinator around existing packages instead of inventing extra DTO layers.
- Skimmed the boundary-smell guidance and identified the main risk: leaking run state/worker details upward into `app` or sideways into `httpserver`.
- Next I am checking story/task references for assumptions about coordinator ownership so the plan names the right package and interfaces.

- Created the task-02 plan at `.ralph/tasks/story-02-control-plane/task-02-run-coordinator_plans/2026-04-30-run-coordinator-plan.md` and linked it from the task file.
- The plan chooses a new `internal/benchmarkrun` package, keeps option alter semantics in `internal/benchmark`, and exposes a direct state snapshot for later JSON serialization without extra DTOs.
- Marked the plan `NOW EXECUTE`; the next turn should read it, execute via TDD vertical slices, and switch back to `TO BE VERIFIED` immediately if the runner/state boundary proves wrong during implementation.

- Read the required TDD and improve-code-boundaries skills plus the prior Ralph progress log.
- Confirmed the selected task is `.ralph/tasks/story-02-control-plane/task-02-run-coordinator.md` and prior progress says its plan is already marked `NOW EXECUTE`.
- Next I am reading the task file and linked plan, then I will execute in vertical TDD slices and switch back to `TO BE VERIFIED` immediately if the design boundary proves wrong.

- Read the task file and linked plan; the plan ends with `NOW EXECUTE`, so this turn must implement rather than create a new plan.
- Confirmed the intended boundary: `internal/benchmark` owns option semantics, and a new `internal/benchmarkrun` package should own lifecycle state and worker coordination.
- Next I am reading the current benchmark package and adjacent code so I can start with the first failing coordinator test on the real existing interfaces.

- Read the existing `internal/benchmark`, `internal/app`, and `internal/httpserver` code plus tests.
- Confirmed there is no existing coordinator yet, and current server/app code should not need functional changes for this task if the new package stays isolated.
- Next I am reading the full options helpers and then I will add the first failing `internal/benchmarkrun` test for `idle -> running`.

- Implemented the initial `internal/benchmarkrun` package and drove it with vertical TDD slices for start, single-active-run rejection, stop/stopping/stopped, alter, and worker failure.
- Moved runtime alter semantics into `benchmark.StartOptions.ApplyAlter` so the coordinator no longer hand-edits option fields.
- Next I am reviewing the new code for boundary mud, then I will run broader tests and finally the required `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test` gates.

- Broader `go test ./...` is green after adding `internal/benchmarkrun` and the benchmark option apply-alter helper.
- I reviewed the coordinator boundary and it still matches the plan: `benchmark` owns option semantics and `benchmarkrun` owns orchestration/state.
- Next I am formatting the touched files, rerunning focused tests, and then running the required make-based gates.

- Completed story-02 task-02: added `internal/benchmarkrun` with explicit run states, runner/run contracts, in-memory lifecycle coordination, stop cancellation, idempotent stop, alter forwarding, and visible failure state.
- Kept the boundary clean by moving runtime alter application into `benchmark.StartOptions.ApplyAlter`; no HTTP or persistence layers were added.
- Required gates passed: `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test`. Next I am running `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, then I will stage everything, commit with test evidence, and push.
